title: Formation of a Porous Structure of Lithium Metal Electrode Under Long-Term Cycling in a Sulfolane Solution of Lithium Bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide
authors: Regina V. Khasanova, Elena V. Karaseva and Vladimir S. Kolosnitsyn
abstract: Long-term cathodic-anodic cycling (more than 570 cycles) of symmetrical lithium-lithium cells with a 1 M solution of LiN(SO2F)2 in sulfolane was studied at a current density of 0.2 mA cm−2, with a metallic lithium plating/stripping capacity of 1 mAh cm−2 and a temperature of 30 °C. An SEM study of the morphology and structure of lithium electrodes revealed that during cathodic-anodic cycling, a 100 μm-thick foil lithium metal electrode transforms into a three-layer structure with an overall thickness of about 250 μm. The outer layer, approximately 60–70 μm thick and adjacent to the separator, …
